name: Discord Bot Bridge
description: Control servers from Discord via slash commands (/servers, /status, /power, /say) over HTTP interactions.
author: Yuno Digital

info.label: Step 1 — Interactions Endpoint URL
info.description: Fill in the Public Key, Application ID and Bot Token below and Save. Then paste this URL into Discord Developer Portal → your app → General Information → Interactions Endpoint URL and Save there — Discord sends a signed ping to verify it, so the Public Key must match exactly.

info.label: Step 2 — Register the slash commands
info.description: Run this once on the panel server after saving the settings. Set a Guild ID below and the commands appear instantly — without it they register globally and can take up to an hour to show up in Discord. Re-run it whenever you change the Guild ID or the commands.

settings.label: Discord Public Key
settings.description: Developer Portal → your application → General Information. Must match, or Discord can't verify the endpoint.

settings.label: Application ID
settings.description: Developer Portal → General Information → Application ID.

settings.label: Bot Token
settings.description: Developer Portal → Bot → Reset Token. Used to register the slash commands.

settings.label: Allowed Discord user IDs
settings.description: Comma-separated Discord user IDs who may run the commands. Enable Developer Mode in Discord, then right-click a user → Copy User ID.

settings.label: Guild ID (recommended)
settings.description: Your Discord server ID (right-click the server → Copy Server ID). Registers commands to that server so they appear instantly. Leave empty to register globally (up to 1 hour delay).
